introMessage: Jeomwon setup provisions Convex, Google OAuth, Resend, OpenAI, and optional Polar without printing secrets.

steps.title: App URL
steps.description: The dashboard app URL linked from the public web app.

variables.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
variables.details: Use localhost for Next dev. 127.0.0.1 is blocked by Next 16 dev cross-origin checks.

steps.title: Site URL
steps.description: The public marketing site URL used by backend emails and server-side links.

variables.name: SITE_URL
variables.details: Use localhost for local Next dev. Production domains should be set separately in production deployments.

steps.title: Convex
steps.description: Create or reuse a Convex dev cloud deployment, then propagate its URL to the apps.
steps.instructions: The wizard checks ~/.convex/config.json. If missing, it can run npx convex login and resume.

variables.name: CONVEX_URL
variables.details: Written by npx convex dev --once after provisioning.

variables.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL
variables.details: The Convex deployment URL copied from packages/backend/.env.local.

variables.name: CONVEX_SITE_URL
variables.details: The Convex HTTP actions URL used for auth callbacks and Polar webhooks.

steps.title: Convex Auth keys
steps.description: Generate RS256 keys with jose and set Convex Auth deployment env.

variables.name: JWT_PRIVATE_KEY
variables.details: RS256 private key generated locally with jose exportPKCS8.

variables.name: JWKS
variables.details: Public JWKS generated locally with jose exportJWK.

steps.title: Google Authentication
steps.description: Google OAuth lets operators sign in to the dashboard app.
steps.instructions: Create a Google Cloud project, configure the OAuth consent screen, then create an OAuth client.

variables.name: AUTH_GOOGLE_ID
variables.details: The client ID from Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials.
info: Authorized JavaScript origins for local dev: http://localhost:3000 and http://localhost:3001

variables.name: AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET
variables.details: The client secret from the same Google OAuth client.

steps.title: Dev-only Anonymous Authentication
steps.description: Optional dev-only shortcut for browser QA. Never enable in production.

variables.name: AUTH_DEV_ANONYMOUS
variables.details: Set to 1 only on dev deployments and local app dev env.

steps.title: Operator allowlist
steps.description: JEOMWON_ADMIN_EMAILS decides which signed-in accounts may open the admin dashboard.
steps.instructions: Required when domain.config.features.customerAccounts is true, because customers and operators then share one login. Optional otherwise.

variables.name: JEOMWON_ADMIN_EMAILS
variables.details: Comma-separated operator emails, matched case-insensitively against the signed-in account. Convex deployment env only — never exposed to the browser.

steps.requiredMessage: Leaving the allowlist empty keeps today's behavior: every signed-in account is an operator.

steps.title: Resend
steps.description: Resend sends transactional reservation and auth emails.
steps.instructions: Create an account at https://resend.com and get an API key.

variables.name: RESEND_API_KEY
variables.details: Find your API key in the Resend dashboard under API Keys. It starts with re_.

variables.name: RESEND_SENDER_EMAIL_AUTH
variables.details: A verified sender email address used for authentication and reservation emails.

steps.requiredMessage: Skipping Resend leaves lifecycle email in capture mode.

steps.title: OpenAI
steps.description: OpenAI powers the agent runtime when AGENT_RUNTIME=openai.

variables.name: OPENAI_API_KEY
variables.details: Stored only in apps/web/.env.local for local Next route handlers.

variables.name: AGENT_RUNTIME
variables.details: Set to openai when the key is configured, or mock when skipped.

steps.title: Polar
steps.description: Polar billing is shown only when domain.config.features.polar is true.
steps.instructions: Create a Polar organization in sandbox first, then configure a raw webhook payload.

variables.name: POLAR_WEBHOOK_SECRET
variables.details: Create a webhook in Polar Settings > Webhooks. Payload format must be Raw.

variables.name: POLAR_ORGANIZATION_TOKEN
variables.details: Generate an Organization Access Token with product, checkout, customer, subscription, and portal permissions.
